summary: Cross-chain mapping for the Wormhole benchmark. The exploit broke the (verify_signatures account → verified-digest record) binding on Solana; the EVM benchmark models the same trust boundary as a (legacyVerifiedSlot key → guardian-set authority) binding.

contracts.note: Solana program IDs; not addressable by EVM tooling. Modelled on EVM by WormholeCore.sol and TokenBridge.sol.

entity_map.role: Solana Wormhole Core program; verify_signatures lived here. Modelled by WormholeCore.sol.
Solana Wormhole Token Bridge program; complete_wrapped lived here. Modelled by TokenBridge.sol.
Ethereum Wormhole Core; verifies guardian-signed VAAs for the redeem path.
Ethereum Wormhole Token Bridge; redeems whETH back into native WETH.
19 guardians (at incident time); their ECDSA signatures are the canonical authority any VAA verifier must check.

asset_map.notes: 120,000 whETH was minted on Solana; ~93,750 was bridged back to Ethereum WETH. whETH on Solana uses 8 decimals; native WETH on Ethereum uses 18 — bridge re-scales by 10^10 on transfer.

invariants_for_fuzzer.description: Every accepted VAA digest must have ≥ guardianQuorum[index] valid ECDSA signatures from guardianSets[index].
legacyVerifiedSlot[k] may only be written when k corresponds to the canonical guardian-set verification record (modelled Sysvar Instructions binding).
TokenBridge.totalMinted across all wrapped assets must equal the total locked native value on the corresponding source-chain bridge minus fees.
TokenBridge.completed[digest] transitions false→true at most once per digest.
